01

Projects

Masters Thesis · Ongoing 2025–2026
Lab-On-A-Disc DNA Analysis System

Designing a centrifugal microfluidic platform for automated DNA extraction — combining magnetophoresis, magnetic bead-based separation, and a custom mechatronic actuation system. Targeting point-of-care diagnostics for the MedTech sector.

Centrifugal Microfluidics Magnetophoresis DNA Extraction Mechatronic Design SolidWorks Dublin City University
Project 01 · Healthcare

Smart Wheelchair with Multi-Mode Control

Designed and 3D-modelled an adjustable LIDAR mount and Raspberry Pi enclosure in SolidWorks. Collaborated with the motor team to synchronise motors via Arduino IDE, enabling three distinct control modes — manual joystick, voice command, and autonomous navigation.

SolidWorksArduinoLIDARRaspberry PiPrototyping
🥇 1st Place · NITTE Healthcare Innovation Hackathon · ₹50,000 prize
Project 02 · AgriTech

Soil Nutrient Testing Device

Designed a device to read soil macronutrients and suggest growable plants. Full 3D CAD in SolidWorks, fabricated via 3D printing. Achieved 90% accuracy against laboratory reference testing.

SolidWorks3D PrintingSensorsSustainability
🥈 2nd Place · Magnovite National Inter-Collegiate Competition, Christ University
Project 03 · Automotive

Smart Tire Inflation Control System

Automatic tire pressure management system adjusting inflation based on drive mode. Designed the weldment frame CAD in SolidWorks, built the full working prototype, and integrated all electronic circuitry.

SolidWorksWeldmentElectronicsPrototyping
📋 Provisional Patent Secured
Project 04 · Product Design

Sanitary Pad Vending Machine

Led a multidisciplinary team to design and build a coin-operated vending machine. Designed in SolidWorks using sheet metal features. 24-pad capacity with Arduino-controlled dispensing and refill alert system.

SolidWorksSheet MetalArduinoTeam Lead
Fully functional working prototype — 40% improvement in user satisfaction
Project 05 · Robotics

Curio — Campus Navigation Robot

Designed the robot body cover in SolidWorks and laser-cut it from acrylic sheets. Assembled the metal frame using power tools. Delivered a functional navigation robot with touch interface deployed in the college administrative block.

SolidWorksLaser CuttingAcrylic FabRobotics
Live deployment in college — touch interface navigation
